She is known in Australia for the [ABC](/wiki/ABC_TV_(Australian_TV_channel) "ABC TV (Australian TV channel)") drama *[The Newsreader](/wiki/The_Newsreader "The Newsreader")*, for which she won two consecutive [AACTA Awards](/wiki/AACTA_Awards "AACTA Awards"). **Anna Torv** (born 7 June 1979) is an Australian actress. Her performance as [Olivia Dunham](/wiki/Olivia_Dunham "Olivia Dunham") in the [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") science fiction series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")* (2008–2013) earned her four consecutive [Saturn Awards for Best Actress on Television](/wiki/Saturn_Award_for_Best_Actress_on_Television "Saturn Award for Best Actress on Television"), a record for any performer. She portrayed psychologist Wendy Carr in the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") period crime drama *[Mindhunter](/wiki/Mindhunter_(TV_series) "Mindhunter (TV series)")* (2017–2019) and [Tess](/wiki/Characters_of_The_Last_of_Us_(TV_series)#Tess "Characters of The Last of Us (TV series)") in the [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") post-apocalyptic drama series *[The Last of Us](/wiki/The_Last_of_Us_(TV_series) "The Last of Us (TV series)")* (2023). Her work on *The Last of Us* garnered her a [Primetime Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Awards "Primetime Emmy Awards") nomination for [Outstanding Guest Actress in a Drama Series](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Guest_Actress_in_a_Drama_Series "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Guest Actress in a Drama Series"). She is known in Australia for the [ABC](/wiki/ABC_TV_(Australian_TV_channel) "ABC TV (Australian TV channel)") drama *[The Newsreader](/wiki/The_Newsreader "The Newsreader")*, for which she won two consecutive [AACTA Awards](/wiki/AACTA_Awards "AACTA Awards"). She stars in the Netflix drama series *[Territory](/wiki/Territory_(TV_series) "Territory (TV series)"),* set in Australia's [Northern Territory](/wiki/Northern_Territory "Northern Territory"), which premiered in October 2024.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Anna_Torv&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Torv was born in [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne")[[1]](#cite_note-secret2016-1) on 7 June 1979,[[2]](#cite_note-2) the daughter of Susan (née Carmichael), of Scottish descent, and Hans Arvid Torv (Tõrv), a property developer and radio presenter[[3]](#cite_note-moore2016-3) of Estonian ancestry.[[4]](#cite_note-Pantagraph-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) At age six, she moved with her mother and younger brother to the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland"), where she grew up,[[1]](#cite_note-secret2016-1) attending [Benowa State High School](/wiki/Benowa_State_High_School "Benowa State High School"), from which she graduated in 1996.[[6]](#cite_note-Perth_Now-6) As of 2008, Torv had been estranged from her father for many years.[[7]](#cite_note-smh2008-7)[[3]](#cite_note-moore2016-3)

Torv's paternal aunt, journalist [Anna dePeyster](/wiki/Anna_dePeyster "Anna dePeyster"), was married to media mogul [Rupert Murdoch](/wiki/Rupert_Murdoch "Rupert Murdoch") for 31 years. Through that marriage, Torv is a cousin of [Elisabeth](/wiki/Elisabeth_Murdoch_(businesswoman) "Elisabeth Murdoch (businesswoman)"), [Lachlan](/wiki/Lachlan_Murdoch "Lachlan Murdoch"), and [James Murdoch](/wiki/James_Murdoch "James Murdoch").[[7]](#cite_note-smh2008-7)

Following her high school graduation, Torv relocated to [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), New South Wales, where she had been accepted into the [National Institute of Dramatic Art](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Dramatic_Art "National Institute of Dramatic Art") (NIDA) at the age of 17. As she was too young to get an electricity account on her own, or to go to pubs, she decided to take a "[gap year](/wiki/Gap_year "Gap year")", during which she worked as cafe and bar staff.[[1]](#cite_note-secret2016-1) She graduated with a [performing arts](/wiki/Performing_arts "Performing arts") degree in 2001.[[7]](#cite_note-smh2008-7)[[1]](#cite_note-secret2016-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Anna_Torv&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bf/Anna_Torv_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg/250px-Anna_Torv_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg)](/wiki/File:Anna_Torv_by_Gage_Skidmore.jpg)

Torv in July 2010

In 2003, Torv played the role of [Ophelia](/wiki/Ophelia "Ophelia") with the [Bell Shakespeare Company](/wiki/Bell_Shakespeare_Company "Bell Shakespeare Company") in [John Bell](/wiki/John_Bell_(Australian_actor) "John Bell (Australian actor)")'s production of *[Hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet "Hamlet")*.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 2004, she joined the cast of Australia's acclaimed television drama *[The Secret Life of Us](/wiki/The_Secret_Life_of_Us "The Secret Life of Us")*, playing Nikki Martel.[[9]](#cite_note-10play2023-9)

In 2005, Torv recorded a series of audio books for [Scholastic Australia](/wiki/Scholastic_Corporation "Scholastic Corporation")'s Solo Collection, including the titles *Little Fingers*, *Jack's Owl*, *Spike*, and *Maddy in the Middle*,[[10]](#cite_note-10) and later did voice work and performance capture for the role of Nariko in the 2007 video game *[Heavenly Sword](/wiki/Heavenly_Sword "Heavenly Sword").*[[11]](#cite_note-11)

She then appeared in the [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") series *[Mistresses](/wiki/Mistresses_(British_TV_series) "Mistresses (British TV series)")* in 2008. From 2008 to 2013, Torv played Agent [Olivia Dunham](/wiki/Olivia_Dunham "Olivia Dunham") in the American television series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")*. She received an [Australians in Film](/wiki/Australians_in_Film "Australians in Film") [Breakthrough award](/wiki/Australians_in_Film#Breakthrough_Award_winners "Australians in Film") in 2009. She has been nominated five times for the [Saturn Award](/wiki/Saturn_Award "Saturn Award") for Best Television Actress from 2009 to 2013, winning a total of four times.[[12]](#cite_note-12) Torv appeared as [Virginia Grey](/wiki/Virginia_Grey "Virginia Grey") on [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO")'s mini-series *[The Pacific](/wiki/The_Pacific_(miniseries) "The Pacific (miniseries)")* and later starred in a *[CollegeHumor](/wiki/CollegeHumor "CollegeHumor") Original* video as a tyrannical traffic cop.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In 2014, Torv reprised her role as Nariko in the [film adaptation](/wiki/Heavenly_Sword_(film) "Heavenly Sword (film)") of *Heavenly Sword*.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In March 2016, Torv was cast in the role of Dr. Wendy Carr, an [FBI](/wiki/Federal_Bureau_of_Investigation "Federal Bureau of Investigation") consultant, in the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") drama *[Mindhunter](/wiki/Mindhunter_(TV_series) "Mindhunter (TV series)")*.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

In July 2021, Torv was cast in the role of [Tess](/wiki/Characters_of_The_Last_of_Us_(TV_series)#Tess "Characters of The Last of Us (TV series)") in the HBO post-apocalyptic series *[The Last of Us](/wiki/The_Last_of_Us_(TV_series) "The Last of Us (TV series)")*.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) The show premiered in January 2023; despite being a guest role, Torv's performance was seen as an early highlight, with Bernard Boo of *[Den of Geek](/wiki/Den_of_Geek "Den of Geek")* writing: "With just about an episode and a half, the terrific Anna Torv leaves an indelible impression as Joel's no-nonsense ride-or-die Tess before literally exiting the series with a bang [...]".[[20]](#cite_note-20) For the series, she was nominated for a [Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Guest Actress in a Drama Series](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Guest_Actress_in_a_Drama_Series "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Guest Actress in a Drama Series"), marking her first ever Emmy nomination.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

Torv played newsreader Helen Norville in the [ABC](/wiki/ABC_TV_(Australian_TV_channel) "ABC TV (Australian TV channel)") series *[The Newsreader](/wiki/The_Newsreader "The Newsreader")*, released in August 2021, which was set in an Australian TV newsroom in the mid-1980s.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The series returned for a second season in November 2023.[[23]](#cite_note-23) Torv won the [AACTA Award](/wiki/AACTA_Awards "AACTA Awards") for Best Lead Actress in a Drama Series three times for her performance as Norville, once for each season.[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25) For the first season, she won the 2022 [Logie Award for Most Outstanding Actress](/wiki/Logie_Award_for_Most_Outstanding_Actress "Logie Award for Most Outstanding Actress"),[[26]](#cite_note-26) and the series also won the [Logie Award for Most Outstanding Drama Series](/wiki/Logie_Award_for_Most_Outstanding_Drama_Series "Logie Award for Most Outstanding Drama Series").[[27]](#cite_note-27) She was nominated for a second Logie for the second season.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

In 2021, Torv also appeared in the television series *[Fires](/wiki/Fires_(TV_series) "Fires (TV series)")*, produced by the ABC Television network, about the [2019–20 Australian bushfire season](/wiki/2019%E2%80%9320_Australian_bushfire_season "2019–20 Australian bushfire season"), in which [megafires](/wiki/Megafire "Megafire") devastated Australia.[[29]](#cite_note-connaughton2021-29)

In October 2024, Torv appeared in the Netflix series *[Territory](/wiki/Territory_(TV_series) "Territory (TV series)")*.[[30]](#cite_note-slatter2024-30)

On 20 November 2025, Torv was named a lead in the new ABC series *[Dustfall](/wiki/Dustfall "Dustfall")*.[[31]](#cite_note-31)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Anna_Torv&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In December 2008, Torv married American actor [Mark Valley](/wiki/Mark_Valley "Mark Valley"), with whom she co-starred in *Fringe*.[[32]](#cite_note-32) In April 2010, it was reported that the couple had separated several months previously.[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34)

Torv lived in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") for over a decade, up until shortly after the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") began in early 2020.[[35]](#cite_note-youwont-35) During her time there, she became a mother and bought a house.[[36]](#cite_note-ww2023-36) She then sold her home and returned to Australia's [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland") in early 2020,[[35]](#cite_note-youwont-35) where she stayed with her mother before moving to [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne "Melbourne") to film *The Newsreader*.[[29]](#cite_note-connaughton2021-29)

As of 2021[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Anna_Torv&action=edit), Torv does not use [social media](/wiki/Social_media "Social media").[[35]](#cite_note-youwont-35) As of January 2024[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Anna_Torv&action=edit), Torv lives in northern [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales").[[37]](#cite_note-thegruellingshoot-37)
